Requirement: Have the codebase organized into shared folders (/helpers, /agents, /workflow, /aggregation)
Value: To find and maintain pipeline components quickly and consistently
Acceptance Criteria: Folder structure exists and imports resolve; build succeeds

Requirement: Have shell command execution and SSH access in one shared module (ShellCommandExecutor)
Value: To run remediation commands on the target VM from any agent that needs it
Acceptance Criteria: Executor runs commands via SSH; read_file and write_file available; no duplicate executor code

Requirement: Have scan logic in one shared module so agents can trigger OpenSCAP scans
Value: To verify fixes and check compliance from Remedy and QA without duplicating scan code
Acceptance Criteria: Scan can be invoked for a finding; returns pass/fail and output; used by Remedy/QA

Requirement: Have a shared LLM wrapper so agents can call the same API and tool pattern
Value: To keep model and API configuration in one place and reuse across Triage, Remedy, Review, QA
Acceptance Criteria: One LLM base used by multiple agents; config (e.g. OpenRouter) in one place

Requirement: Have shared utilities (e.g. normalization, error handling) in one module
Value: To avoid duplicate logic and keep behavior consistent across agents
Acceptance Criteria: Utilities used by at least two agents or pipeline; tests exist for shared utils

Requirement: Have workflow data models (TriageDecision, RemediationAttempt, ReviewVerdict, QAInput, etc.) in schemas
Value: So every agent and the pipeline agree on inputs and outputs
Acceptance Criteria: Schemas define all pipeline types; pipeline and agents import and use them

Requirement: Have agent class stubs (Triage, Remedy, Review, QA) with a common process() interface
Value: To implement and test each agent in isolation and plug them into the pipeline
Acceptance Criteria: Four agent modules exist; each has process(input) returning the right output type

Requirement: Have a pipeline class stub that runs one finding through the stages
Value: To wire Triage → Remedy → Review → QA in order and return a single result
Acceptance Criteria: Pipeline.run(vulnerability) exists; calls each agent in sequence; returns FindingResult

Requirement: Have a concurrency manager stub that can run multiple pipelines
Value: To later run many findings in parallel without blocking
Acceptance Criteria: Manager accepts a list of findings and a pipeline factory; placeholder for worker pool

Requirement: Have a result aggregator stub that collects pipeline results
Value: To later produce one report and one playbook from all findings
Acceptance Criteria: Aggregator accepts list of FindingResult; stub returns or writes a minimal report

Requirement: Have a single entrypoint (e.g. main_multiagent.py) that runs the pipeline from scan or parsed input
Value: To run the full flow with one command instead of separate scripts
Acceptance Criteria: Entrypoint runs; can use inventory and scan or skip-scan with parsed JSON; produces outputs

Requirement: Have the legacy adaptive agent moved into a reference location (e.g. legacy_files)
Value: To keep the old behavior available without blocking the new pipeline
Acceptance Criteria: Legacy code moved; imports updated or documented; main pipeline does not depend on it

Requirement: Have all import paths and module wiring updated after restructure
Value: So the project builds and runs without broken imports
Acceptance Criteria: No import errors; pipeline runs one finding end-to-end in a test

Requirement: Have build and runtime verified after restructure
Value: To confirm nothing was broken by the refactor
Acceptance Criteria: Build succeeds; one full pipeline run completes; no runtime errors from structure

Requirement: Have vulnerability scan output parsed into a structured list (e.g. Vulnerability) for the pipeline
Value: To prioritize and process only real findings from the scan
Acceptance Criteria: Parser consumes OpenSCAP XML (or equivalent); outputs list of findings with id, title, severity, etc.

Requirement: Have each finding classified by risk (e.g. safe to remediate, human review, too dangerous)
Value: So only appropriate findings are auto-remediated and the rest are flagged
Acceptance Criteria: Triage returns a decision per finding; classification is repeatable and logged

Requirement: Have findings that need human review flagged so they are not auto-remediated
Value: To avoid automating high-risk changes without oversight
Acceptance Criteria: Human-review findings are not sent to Remedy; they appear in reports for manual handling

Requirement: Have Triage output a structured TriageDecision (e.g. should_remediate, risk_level, reason)
Value: So the pipeline and downstream agents get a consistent triage result
Acceptance Criteria: TriageDecision model exists and is returned by Triage agent; used by pipeline and Remedy

Requirement: Have triage decisions covered by unit tests so behavior is stable across changes
Value: To trust that triage logic still matches requirements after code changes
Acceptance Criteria: Unit tests exist for Triage; tests cover at least heuristic and one LLM path; tests pass

Requirement: Have the Remedy agent use run_cmd, read_file, write_file, and scan tools only
Value: To limit what the agent can do on the VM and keep actions auditable
Acceptance Criteria: Remedy only calls these four tools; no direct shell or undocumented tools

Requirement: Have a core remediation loop that runs commands and then verifies with a scan
Value: So each fix is checked before moving to Review
Acceptance Criteria: Remedy runs commands, then runs scan for that finding; scan result is in RemediationAttempt

Requirement: Have retry logic and a max-attempt limit when the scan still fails
Value: To avoid infinite loops and surface hard findings for manual handling
Acceptance Criteria: After N failed attempts, Remedy stops; previous attempts are available for feedback

Requirement: Have Remedy integration coordinated with Triage, Review, and QA (inputs/outputs)
Value: So the pipeline passes the right data between stages
Acceptance Criteria: Remedy receives TriageDecision and vulnerability; outputs RemediationAttempt used by Review/QA

Requirement: Have the Review agent evaluate whether the remediation is a good solution (not just working)
Value: To approve only quality fixes and send weak ones back for improvement
Acceptance Criteria: Review returns a verdict (e.g. approve, feedback, concerns); verdict is used by pipeline

Requirement: Have an optimality analysis (e.g. minimal change, best practices) in the review step
Value: So remediations are not only correct but also maintainable and safe
Acceptance Criteria: Review prompt and output include optimality/quality criteria; feedback is actionable

Requirement: Have a review → remedy feedback loop so rejected fixes can be retried with suggestions
Value: To improve fix quality without manual re-runs
Acceptance Criteria: When Review rejects, pipeline can send feedback to Remedy and retry (within limits)

Requirement: Have Review output a structured ReviewVerdict (e.g. approve, feedback, concerns)
Value: So the pipeline and QA get a consistent review result
Acceptance Criteria: ReviewVerdict model exists and is returned by Review agent; used by pipeline and QA

Requirement: Have the review–remedy loop behavior validated (approve path and reject/retry path)
Value: To confirm the pipeline does not get stuck and respects max retries
Acceptance Criteria: Test or scenario shows approve → QA and reject → Remedy retry; loop exits correctly

Requirement: Have the QA agent check that key services are still available after remediation
Value: To ensure fixes did not break the system
Acceptance Criteria: QA runs service checks (e.g. systemctl status); result is part of QAResult

Requirement: Have QA output a structured QAResult (e.g. safe, recommendation, system_checks)
Value: So the pipeline and aggregation get a consistent QA result
Acceptance Criteria: QAResult model exists and is returned by QA agent; used by pipeline and reports

Requirement: Have regression detection so we know if a fix broke another finding or the system
Value: To catch side effects before marking a finding as done
Acceptance Criteria: QA or pipeline can detect regressions; result is visible in report or QAResult

Requirement: Have validation that safe vs unsafe remediation cases are handled correctly
Value: To trust that the system only approves when QA says safe
Acceptance Criteria: Test cases for safe and unsafe outcomes; pipeline status and report reflect QA verdict

Requirement: Have structured JSON outputs for evaluation and reporting
Value: To analyze results and compare runs without parsing logs by hand
Acceptance Criteria: Pipeline or aggregator writes JSON (e.g. findings, results, status); format is documented

Requirement: Have aggregation logic that combines all finding results into one report and playbook
Value: To see mission readiness and risk in one place
Acceptance Criteria: Aggregator produces consolidated report (e.g. counts, list of results) and optional playbook

Requirement: Have an overall evaluation plan (internal and UAT) documented
Value: To run tests and UAT in a consistent way
Acceptance Criteria: Document describes what will be tested, how, and what success means (e.g. 75% remediation)

Requirement: Have a system block diagram for the multi-agent architecture
Value: To explain the system to stakeholders and new team members
Acceptance Criteria: Diagram shows main components (agents, pipeline, helpers, aggregation) and relationships

Requirement: Have a data flow diagram for single-finding pipeline execution
Value: To show how data moves from scan to report
Acceptance Criteria: Diagram shows inputs/outputs and flow for one finding through Triage → Remedy → Review → QA

Requirement: Have a logging and audit framework design for agent workflows
Value: To trace decisions and actions for compliance and debugging
Acceptance Criteria: Design document or diagram describes what is logged and where; aligns with DISA STIG / NIST

Requirement: Have requirements and feature definitions finalized for each agent
Value: So implementation and acceptance criteria are aligned
Acceptance Criteria: Document or backlog lists each agent’s responsibility, inputs, outputs, and success criteria

Requirement: Have a definition of success for each agent interaction (acceptance + DoD)
Value: To know when a story or sprint is done (e.g. 75% remediation rate)
Acceptance Criteria: Each agent has acceptance criteria and DoD; sprint DoD includes 75% remediation on evaluation set

Requirement: Have a decision log for implementation assumptions (e.g. STIG scope, tool limits)
Value: To explain design choices to the sponsor and future maintainers
Acceptance Criteria: Log exists with at least 5 entries; accessible to the team and sponsor

Requirement: Have alignment between team output and sponsor expectations (e.g. DISA STIG Red Hat)
Value: To avoid building the wrong product
Acceptance Criteria: Sponsor or PM has confirmed that scope and priorities match (e.g. Red Hat STIG support)

Requirement: Have Sprint 1 milestone tracking (design and implementation)
Value: To report progress and adjust plan
Acceptance Criteria: Milestones or checklist exist; actual progress is updated at least once per sprint

Requirement: Have the Gantt chart updated with agent-based implementation tasks
Value: To see schedule and dependencies in one place
Acceptance Criteria: Gantt reflects current WBS and sprint boundaries; agent and pipeline tasks are visible

Requirement: Have actual vs. planned progress reported (e.g. in status or backlog)
Value: To spot delays and reallocate work
Acceptance Criteria: Progress is visible (e.g. in Jira or a status doc); at least sprint-level accuracy

Requirement: Have the risk log updated with current risks and mitigations
Value: To manage project and technical risk
Acceptance Criteria: Risk log has entries; each has mitigation or owner; updated during the sprint

Requirement: Have mitigation strategies documented for each identified risk
Value: To act on risks instead of only listing them
Acceptance Criteria: Each major risk has a short mitigation; doc is in repo or project space

Requirement: Have initial test cases for remediation success and failure scenarios
Value: To validate the pipeline before UAT
Acceptance Criteria: At least 3 test cases (e.g. success, scan fail, review reject); cases are runnable and documented

Requirement: Have an internal evaluation plan (unit, integration, system) documented
Value: To run internal tests in a repeatable way
Acceptance Criteria: Plan lists test levels, scope, and pass criteria; team can execute it

Requirement: Have a user acceptance test plan (protocol, users, metrics) documented
Value: To run UAT with real users later
Acceptance Criteria: UAT plan describes who, what they will do, what we measure, and what “pass” means

Requirement: Have a configurable worker pool (e.g. max 4 workers) for concurrent pipeline runs
Value: To process multiple findings in parallel and tune load on the VM
Acceptance Criteria: Concurrency is configurable (e.g. 1–4); multiple findings run in parallel; no deadlock or corruption

Requirement: Have finding queue distribution so each worker gets findings until the backlog is done
Value: To balance work across workers and finish all findings
Acceptance Criteria: All findings are processed; no finding is dropped or run twice; completion order is logged

Requirement: Have shared VM coordination so concurrent runs do not conflict on the same host
Value: To avoid overlapping commands or scan runs on one VM
Acceptance Criteria: Concurrent runs complete without VM errors; scans and commands do not interfere in tests

Requirement: Have concurrent execution correctness validated (results match sequential behavior)
Value: To trust that concurrency does not change outcomes
Acceptance Criteria: Same findings run sequential vs concurrent; aggregated results are equivalent (e.g. same success count)

Requirement: Have a stress test with many findings (e.g. 10+) running concurrently
Value: To ensure stability and performance under load
Acceptance Criteria: Stress test runs; no crashes or hangs; system completes within a reasonable time; 75% DoD still measurable

Requirement: Have aggregation logic that combines results from all concurrent runs into one report
Value: To get one report and one playbook for the full run
Acceptance Criteria: Aggregator consumes all FindingResults; produces one report and one playbook; counts are correct

Requirement: Have acceptance criteria defined for each agent’s behavior (for tests and UAT)
Value: To test and accept each agent in isolation and in the pipeline
Acceptance Criteria: Each agent has 2+ acceptance criteria; criteria are testable and written in user/customer terms

Requirement: Have outcomes classified (e.g. success, retry exhaustion, failure) in the report
Value: To see how many findings were fixed, failed, or discarded
Acceptance Criteria: Report or JSON includes outcome per finding and summary counts; classification is consistent

Requirement: Have traceability across findings and agents (e.g. which agent did what for which finding)
Value: To debug and audit the pipeline
Acceptance Criteria: Logs or report allow tracing one finding through Triage → Remedy → Review → QA

Requirement: Have the Remedy agent refactored for clarity and reuse (e.g. tools, loop, scan)
Value: To reduce bugs and make future changes easier
Acceptance Criteria: Remedy code is cleaner; behavior unchanged; tests still pass

Requirement: Have the Review agent refactored for clarity and reuse
Value: To reduce bugs and make future changes easier
Acceptance Criteria: Review code is cleaner; behavior unchanged; tests still pass

Requirement: Have the pipeline and agents deployed and running against Red Hat standard (DISA STIG)
Value: To meet the sponsor requirement for Red Hat STIG support
Acceptance Criteria: System runs on a Red Hat–based image with STIG content; scan and remediation use STIG profile

Requirement: Have test files (e.g. configs, broken VM, non–Red Hat compliant) generated for evaluation
Value: To run internal and UAT tests under different conditions
Acceptance Criteria: At least 2–3 test scenarios (e.g. compliant VM, non-compliant, broken); files or scripts are documented

Requirement: Have integration test cases and scripts that run the pipeline with real components
Value: To catch integration bugs before UAT
Acceptance Criteria: Integration tests exist; they run pipeline with real agents; at least one full run passes

Requirement: Have system test cases and scripts that run end-to-end with realistic data
Value: To validate the full system before UAT
Acceptance Criteria: System tests exist; they run from entrypoint to report; pass criteria include 75% remediation where applicable

Requirement: Have a user manual that explains how to install, configure, and run the system
Value: To hand off the system to operators and evaluators
Acceptance Criteria: Manual has install, config, and run sections; at least one person outside the team can run the pipeline using it

Requirement: Have a way to evaluate performance for different models (e.g. swap LLM and compare)
Value: To benchmark and choose models for deployment
Acceptance Criteria: Process or script allows swapping model (e.g. via env); at least 2 configs run and results are comparable

Requirement: Have UAT test cases ready for real users (e.g. 20 participants)
Value: To run user acceptance testing and collect feedback
Acceptance Criteria: UAT cases are written; they map to user stories and acceptance criteria; protocol is clear

Requirement: Have different-systems tests (e.g. different OS or scan profiles) if in scope
Value: To ensure the system works in more than one environment
Acceptance Criteria: If in scope: tests run on at least 2 configurations; results are documented. If out of scope: N/A noted

Requirement: Have human-in-the-loop workflow evaluation (e.g. findings sent to human review)
Value: To validate that human-review path works and is observable
Acceptance Criteria: Human-review findings are visible and can be reviewed; workflow does not auto-remediate them

Requirement: Have reporting/aggregation correctness tests (counts, playbook content)
Value: To trust that the report and playbook match pipeline results
Acceptance Criteria: Tests check that report counts and playbook tasks match FindingResults; tests pass
